Title: Enzymes in 3D: Synthesis, remodelling, and hydrolysis of cell wall (1,3;1,4)-β-glucans

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dietary fiber with human health benefits
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Possible but uncertain contribution to (1,3;1,4)-β-glucan synthesis; direct role remains doubtful
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcript profiling and cited prior functional studies
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Substantial early peak of transcription during barley grain development
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review explicitly notes remaining doubt as to whether CslF9 is directly involved in (1,3;1,4)-β-glucan synthesis; therefore not treated as primary pathway determinant in this paper
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CslF9 shows an early transcription peak during barley grain development. 2. It is discussed as a candidate related to β-glucan synthesis. 3. Its direct enzymatic role remains unresolved.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential secondary target pending functional clarification

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cellulose synthase-like F6 (CslF6), GT2 family processive glycosyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported; CslF6 is described as the prime target and major synthase confirmed repeatedly for cereal grain (1,3;1,4)-β-glucan synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucosyl residues from UDP-Glc to the nonreducing end of an elongating glucan chain, inserting both β-(1,4) and single β-(1,3) linkages into the nascent (1,3;1,4)-β-glucan
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dietary fiber associated with reduced risk of type II diabetes, colorectal cancer, and cardiovascular disease; may beneficially alter gut microbiome
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Cell wall, especially grain starchy endosperm; also leaf walls in overexpression lines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes and directly determines (1,3;1,4)-β-glucan accumulation; overexpression increases grain and leaf β-glucan levels and alters DP3:DP4 ratio
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative genomics/QTL and EST mapping; transcript profiling; transgenic overexpression; heterologous expression in Sf9 insect cells; in vitro polysaccharide synthesis and biochemical analysis; cryo-EM structure; mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Golgi apparatus or plasma membrane/secretory membrane system; unresolved and may depend on species, tissue, isoenzyme, and developmental stage
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Most abundant CslF transcript in developing barley endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in developing endosperm; overexpression analyzed in grain and leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro Sf9 insect-cell expression system for mechanistic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A single enzyme is sufficient for synthesis without exogenous primer; likely self-primes via UDP-Glc hydrolysis and released glucose. Cryo-EM/PDB 8dqk showed a CesA-like fold with 7 transmembrane helices and likely monomeric action in vitro. Switch motif MTASCSAYLA, especially Y787, is essential for efficient insertion of β-(1,3) linkages and influences fine structure/DP3:DP4 control, though additional structural determinants are required
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for breeding and metabolic engineering of high-soluble-fiber barley/oat grain; overexpression or fine-tuning of catalytic residues may increase β-glucan amount and tailor DP3:DP4 ratio for health foods, beverages, malting, brewing, and bioenergy applications
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Interpretation of transgenic experiments can be complicated by pleiotropic effects; precise control of DP3:DP4 and solubility remains unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CslF6 is the principal experimentally validated synthase responsible for cereal grain (1,3;1,4)-β-glucan production. 2. A single barley CslF6 expressed in Sf9 insect cells synthesizes mixed-linkage β-glucan up to DP >100 without exogenous primer. 3. Cryo-EM and mutagenesis revealed how one GT2 enzyme can insert both β-(1,3) and β-(1,4) linkages, with Y787 in the switch motif being critical for β-(1,3)-linkage formation.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative genomics based on QTL and EST mapping; transcript profiling in developing endosperm

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Hetero-transglycosylation involving xyloglucan and mixed-linkage β-glucan
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Xyloglucan donor (e.g., XXXG heptasaccharide) and (1,3;1,4)-β-oligoglucoside acceptor (e.g., G4G4G3G)
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Covalently linked hetero-transglycosylation products between xyloglucan and (1,3;1,4)-β-glucan-derived oligosaccharides
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a xyloglucan-derived glycosyl fragment to a (1,3;1,4)-β-glucan oligosaccharide acceptor via transglycosylation with retained stereochemistry
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for remodeling/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall structural polysaccharide and dietary fiber
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports in vivo and in silico capacity for hetero-transglycosylation involving (1,3;1,4)-β-glucan, implying a role in cell-wall remodeling rather than de novo synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Fluorescent oligosaccharide probe infiltration in young barley roots; substrate docking with AlphaFold model; prior enzymology
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: XET transcripts are high in tissues where wall components are synthesized
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Young barley apical root segments/tissues synthesizing wall components
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Biological significance and extent of these reactions during normal growth remain unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AlphaFold-based docking showed barley XET4 can accommodate xyloglucan donor and mixed-linkage β-glucan acceptor substrates. 2. The enzyme is capable of hetero-transglycosylation involving (1,3;1,4)-β-glucan-related oligosaccharides. 3. Its role is inferred in cell-wall remodeling rather than synthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Hetero-transglycosylation between xyloglucan and mixed-linkage β-glucan/cellulosic polysaccharides
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Xyloglucan donor; (1,3;1,4)-β-glucan or cellulosic polysaccharide acceptors
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Covalently linked hybrid wall polysaccharide products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes transglycosylation forming covalent linkages between xyloglucans and either (1,3;1,4)-β-glucans or cellulosic polysaccharides in vitro
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for remodeling/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall structural polysaccharide and dietary fiber
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Demonstrates enzymatic capacity to remodel mixed-linkage β-glucan by forming covalent cross-links with xyloglucan/cellulosic substrates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Highly purified enzyme in vitro transglycosylation assays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: XET transcripts are high in tissues where wall components are synthesized
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Tissues where wall components are synthesized
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Whether these reactions occur extensively in vivo remains unresolved
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Purified barley XET5 forms covalent linkages between xyloglucan and (1,3;1,4)-β-glucan or cellulose-like polysaccharides in vitro. 2. This supports a possible role in mixed-linkage β-glucan remodeling. 3. In vivo biological significance is still uncertain.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Nonreducing termini of (1,3)-β-glucans, (1,6)-β-glucans, (1,3;1,4)-β-glucans, (1,3;1,6)-β-glucans, and β-glucosyl oligosaccharides
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Sequentially removes single glucose residues from the nonreducing ends of diverse β-glucans and β-glucosides with retention of anomeric configuration
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall storage/structural polysaccharide and dietary fiber
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes turnover/hydrolysis of (1,3;1,4)-β-glucan and other β-glucans; exhibits broad specificity and unusual processive catalysis for (1,3)-β-glucans
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Purification and enzyme assays; X-ray crystallography; substrate analogue complexes; molecular dynamics and multiscale modeling; mutational analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Young barley seedlings; functions during growth/development and light/dark periods
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contains catalytic D285 and E491 and W286/W434 aromatic clamp. Substrate-product-assisted processive catalysis applies to (1,3)-β-glucan hydrolysis, whereas hydrolysis of (1,3;1,4)-β-glucan involves dissociation/re-association after each event
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Exo1 is a broad-specificity GH3 β-glucan exohydrolase that hydrolyzes mixed-linkage β-glucan among several β-glucan substrates. 2. Structural and modeling studies uncovered a side-door product egress pathway and substrate-product-assisted processive catalysis. 3. The W286/W434 tryptophan clamp underlies broad specificity, catalytic efficiency, and processivity.

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Characteristic oligosaccharides including DP3 and DP4 fragments
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes internal β-(1,4) linkages located on the reducing-terminal side of a β-(1,3)-linked glucosyl residue in (1,3;1,4)-β-glucan
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall polysaccharide and dietary fiber
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Specifically depolymerizes mixed-linkage β-glucan, defining its fine-structure-derived hydrolysis pattern
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: X-ray crystallography; enzymology; docking of oligosaccharide substrate
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Catalytic residues E93 acid/base and E232 nucleophile; adopts an (α/β)8 barrel with ~40 Å substrate-binding cleft
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EII specifically hydrolyzes mixed-linkage β-glucan but not pure β-(1,3)- or β-(1,4)-glucans. 2. Its structure explains endo action through a long substrate-binding cleft. 3. The enzyme likely evolved from more widespread (1,3)-β-glucanases.
